rationale: The two papers overlap at a broad level (online WOM/content and emotion/positivity, both using LIWC-style text measures), so the 2012 paper could plausibly be a background or theoretical source. However, the citing paper’s core question is incentive-driven changes in review writing/enjoyment, while the cited paper focuses on virality/sharing, and the provided evidence includes only a DOI match with no in-text citation context showing substantive use.
